title: Deal Asset
description: An independent, persistent asset component originating from a deal. DealAssets are the investable units that flow from deal origination into fund portfolios — they are the core components allocated to and held within funds. Each DealAsset has its own identity and lifecycle, independent of the Deal that created it. A DealAsset may be referenced by Deals, Fund Positions, Portfolios, and other entities.

allOf.description: A persistent, independently-tracked asset component that originates from a deal. Each DealAsset carries its own asset class and references a domain-specific entity (Facility, Investment, Property, or LPInterest). DealAssets are the investable building blocks — they are what funds actually hold and what gets allocated across portfolios. A single deal may produce multiple DealAssets across different asset classes (e.g., an LBO produces a senior term loan, a mezz tranche, and an equity co-invest as separate DealAssets). Example: {"id": "da112233-...", "deal_id": "de112233-...", "asset_type": "FACILITY", "asset_class": "PRIVATE_CREDIT", "label": "Senior Term Loan", "facility_id": "...", "notional_amount": {"amount": "500000000", "currency": "USD"}}

deal_id.description: UUID reference to the Deal entity that originated this asset. Links the asset back to its deal context for provenance tracking. A DealAsset always originates from exactly one Deal, but its lifecycle is independent — it can persist and be traded long after the deal closes. Always populated. Example: 'de112233-4455-6677-8899-aabbccdde001'

asset_type.description: Discriminator indicating which domain entity this asset references. Determines which UUID reference field should be populated: FACILITY → facility_id, INVESTMENT → investment_id, PROPERTY → property_id, LP_INTEREST → lp_interest_id. Example: 'FACILITY'

asset_class.description: Broad asset class classification for this specific component of the deal. Each deal asset is independently classified — a single deal may have assets spanning PRIVATE_CREDIT, PRIVATE_EQUITY, PUBLIC_CREDIT, and other classes. Example: 'PRIVATE_CREDIT'

label.description: Human-readable label identifying this asset within the deal. Should be unique within the deal's asset list and descriptive enough to distinguish components (e.g., 'Senior Term Loan A' vs 'Revolving Credit Facility'). Example: 'Senior Term Loan'

facility_id.description: UUID reference to a credit/Facility entity. Populate only when asset_type = FACILITY. Example: 'fa112233-4455-6677-8899-aabbccdde001'

investment_id.description: UUID reference to a fund/Investment (portfolio company equity stake). Populate only when asset_type = INVESTMENT. Example: 'in112233-4455-6677-8899-aabbccdde001'

property_id.description: UUID reference to a real_estate/Property entity. Populate only when asset_type = PROPERTY. Example: 'pr112233-4455-6677-8899-aabbccdde001'

lp_interest_id.description: UUID reference to a secondaries/LPInterest entity. Populate only when asset_type = LP_INTEREST. Example: 'lp112233-4455-6677-8899-aabbccdde001'

notional_amount.description: Notional, face, or par value of this asset component within the deal. For credit facilities, this is the commitment amount. For equity, this is the invested amount. For properties, this is the acquisition price. Example: {"amount": "500000000", "currency": "USD"}
